text:
There was a kid named Gary who found an old, dusty guitar near his parent's attic. One rainy afternoon, he decided to play it, and lovable little birdie called out a silly tune that made Gary's heart sing. That song became his favorite, and every night before bed, he would serenade the moon.

One day, Gary's friend Max came to visit. Instead of playing, Max grabbed the guitar. Gary laughed and said, "That's my baby!"

Max was curious. "How do you play it?"

Gary showed him how to hold it, strum some chords, and flutter his fingers like a bird. They played a few songs together – whimsical tunes about dreams and clouds.

Their little circle of friends played that guitar song that magical rainy day, each time dreaming up new adventures as they sang.

Years later, Gary passed away peacefully, surrounded by laughter and his favorite song. In remembrance, his family made a wish: every time someone heard that joyful
